creator: bsd@j-star.org
text:
Yes, that's intentional.

(The explanation:

I live in a country where copyright in all the code I write - even as a hobby, outside of work hours with personal equipment - can be claimed by my employer. Thankfully, my employer has a liberal open-source contribution policy! (Much more liberal than when I used to work at a university, which is unbelievable ... but that's an aside.) One term of that policy is that commits are associated with a work email address for visibility

My identity online, though, isn't my work email, but *@j-star.org. This has been stable across a number of employers, and will continue to be so. So that's what I use for the bugzilla login etc as my stable "identity".

Hope that makes sense.)
